page.title: Active Learning & Metacognition Demo
page.description: Learn about spaced repetition, active recall, and other cognitive science learning techniques.

sections.title: Introduction to Metacognition
sections.content: Metacognition is **thinking about thinking**. It involves being aware of your own learning processes, monitoring your understanding, and regulating your study methods. This page demonstrates how **Active Recall** and other cognitive strategies can help you learn more efficiently.

cards.back: A learning technique that involves actively retrieving information from memory (e.g., testing yourself) rather than passively reading/reviewing notes.
Reviewing material at increasing intervals over time to exploit the psychological spacing effect and flatten the forgetting curve.
The ability to evaluate and assess your own understanding of a topic to plan your study focus.
A learning method where you explain a concept in simple terms, as if teaching a beginner, to identify gaps in your own understanding.
A theory suggesting that our working memory has a limited capacity, and educational materials should avoid overloading it.

sections.title: Match the Method to Its Practice

pairs.right: Using flashcards and practice questions to test retrieval.
Scheduling review sessions after 1 day, then 3 days, then 1 week.
Evaluating your own understanding to plan your study focus.
